phrases.text_local: Où peut-on louer des petits bateaux pour le bassin ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo puh-TOHN loo-AY day puh-TEE bah-TOH poor luh bah-SAHN
phrases.cultural_note: The toy sailboat rental is at the central octagonal basin. Kids push them with sticks — it's a Parisian tradition since the 1920s. Available spring through fall.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the green chairs are free to use
phrases.cultural_note: Yes! The famous green metal chairs are free. Drag them anywhere you like — to the sun, shade, or near the fountain. It's a beloved Parisian tradition.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Medici Fountain is
phrases.text_local: Où est la fontaine de Médicis ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo eh lah fohn-TEN duh may-dee-SEES
phrases.cultural_note: The Medici Fountain is in the northeast corner, built in 1630 for Marie de Médicis. The long reflecting pool leading to it is one of the most romantic spots in Paris.

phrases.text_local: Il y a une aire de jeux pour les enfants ?
phrases.pronunciation: eel ee ah oon EHR duh ZHUH poor layz ahn-FAHN
phrases.cultural_note: There's a paid playground (a few euros) with slides and swings, and a free puppet theater (Guignol) with shows on Wednesday, Saturday, and Sunday.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the park closes
phrases.text_local: Le jardin ferme à quelle heure ce soir ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh zhar-DAHN FEHRM ah kel UHR suh SWAHR
phrases.cultural_note: Closing time changes with the seasons — check the posted signs at the gates. A bell rings 30 minutes before closing as a warning. Guards will politely usher you out.
